_**User (2025-06-02 18:49:38Z)**_

Convert this address-autocomplete picker to use this code


"use client"
import { Check, ChevronsUpDown, MapPin } from "lucide-react"
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button"
import { Command, CommandEmpty, CommandGroup, CommandInput, CommandItem, CommandList } from "@/components/ui/command"
import { Popover, PopoverContent, PopoverTrigger } from "@/components/ui/popover"
import { useState, useEffect, useRef } from "react"

// Saved locations that will always be available
const savedLocations = [
  { value: "airport", label: "Airport" },
  { value: "port", label: "Port" },
  { value: "athens", label: "Athens" },
  { value: "patra", label: "Patra" },
]

interface LocationPickerProps {
  onLocationSelect?: (location: string) => void
}

export function LocationPicker({ onLocationSelect }: LocationPickerProps) {
  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false)
  const [value, setValue] = useState("")
  const [inputValue, setInputValue] = useState("")
  const [suggestions, setSuggestions] = useState<Array<{ value: string; label: string }>>([])
  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(false)

  // Reference to the Google Maps Places service
  const placesServiceRef = useRef<google.maps.places.AutocompleteService | null>(null)
  const sessionTokenRef = useRef<google.maps.places.AutocompleteSessionToken | null>(null)

  // Initialize Google Maps Places API
  useEffect(() => {
    // Load Google Maps API script
    const script = document.createElement("script")
    script.src = `https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/js?key=${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY}&libraries=places`
    script.async = true
    script.defer = true
    script.onload = () => {
      // Check if google is defined
      if (typeof google !== "undefined") {
        placesServiceRef.current = new google.maps.places.AutocompleteService()
        sessionTokenRef.current = new google.maps.places.AutocompleteSessionToken()
      } else {
        console.error("Google Maps API not loaded")
      }
    }
    document.head.appendChild(script)

    return () => {
      document.head.removeChild(script)
    }
  }, [])

  // Filter saved locations and fetch Google Maps suggestions
  useEffect(() => {
    // First filter saved locations
    const filteredSavedLocations = savedLocations.filter((location) =>
      location.label.toLowerCase().includes(inputValue.toLowerCase()),
    )

    // If we have a search term and no matching saved locations, fetch from Google Maps
    if (inputValue && filteredSavedLocations.length === 0 && placesServiceRef.current) {
      setIsLoading(true)

      placesServiceRef.current.getPlacePredictions(
        {
          input: inputValue,
          sessionToken: sessionTokenRef.current,
        },
        (predictions, status) => {
          setIsLoading(false)

          if (status === google.maps.places.PlacesServiceStatus.OK && predictions) {
            const googleSuggestions = predictions.map((prediction) => ({
              value: prediction.place_id,
              label: prediction.description,
            }))
            setSuggestions([...filteredSavedLocations, ...googleSuggestions])
          } else {
            setSuggestions(filteredSavedLocations)
          }
        },
      )
    } else {
      setSuggestions(filteredSavedLocations)
    }
  }, [inputValue])

  const handleSelect = (currentValue: string, currentLabel: string) => {
    setValue(currentValue)
    setInputValue(currentLabel)
    setOpen(false)
    if (onLocationSelect) {
      onLocationSelect(currentLabel)
    }
  }

  return (
    <Popover open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen}>
      <PopoverTrigger asChild>
        <Button variant="outline" role="combobox" aria-expanded={open} className="w-full justify-between">
          {value
            ? suggestions.find((suggestion) => suggestion.value === value)?.label || inputValue
            : "Select pick up point..."}
          <ChevronsUpDown className="ml-2 h-4 w-4 shrink-0 opacity-50" />
        </Button>
      </PopoverTrigger>
      <PopoverContent className="w-[300px] p-0">
        <Command>
          <CommandInput placeholder="Search location..." value={inputValue} onValueChange={setInputValue} />
          <CommandList>
            <CommandEmpty>{isLoading ? "Loading..." : "No location found."}</CommandEmpty>
            <CommandGroup heading="Saved Locations">
              {suggestions
                .filter((location) => savedLocations.some((saved) => saved.value === location.value))
                .map((suggestion) => (
                  <CommandItem
                    key={suggestion.value}
                    value={suggestion.value}
                    onSelect={(currentValue) => handleSelect(currentValue, suggestion.label)}
                  >
                    <MapPin className="mr-2 h-4 w-4" />
                    {suggestion.label}
                    <Check
                      className={cn("ml-auto h-4 w-4", value === suggestion.value ? "opacity-100" : "opacity-0")}
                    />
                  </CommandItem>
                ))}
            </CommandGroup>
            {suggestions.some((location) => !savedLocations.some((saved) => saved.value === location.value)) && (
              <CommandGroup heading="Suggestions">
                {suggestions
                  .filter((location) => !savedLocations.some((saved) => saved.value === location.value))
                  .map((suggestion) => (
                    <CommandItem
                      key={suggestion.value}
                      value={suggestion.value}
                      onSelect={(currentValue) => handleSelect(currentValue, suggestion.label)}
                    >
                      <MapPin className="mr-2 h-4 w-4" />
                      {suggestion.label}
                      <Check
                        className={cn("ml-auto h-4 w-4", value === suggestion.value ? "opacity-100" : "opacity-0")}
                      />
                    </CommandItem>
                  ))}
              </CommandGroup>
            )}
          </CommandList>
        </Command>
      </PopoverContent>
    </Popover>
  )
}



Make it work with out api and not googlemaps

_**Agent**_

I've converted the address autocomplete component to use the new UI components while maintaining the OpenStreetMap API integration. Here are the key changes I made:

1. Replaced the basic input with the new Command/Popover-based UI components
2. Maintained all the existing OpenStreetMap API functionality
3. Added proper styling and icons from the new design
4. Kept all the existing address data handling and formatting
5. Improved the UI with loading states and better error handling

The new version includes:
- A cleaner, more modern UI using the Command component
- Better keyboard navigation support
- Loading states and empty states
- MapPin icons for each suggestion
- Check marks for selected items
- Proper error and disabled states

The component still maintains all the original functionality:
- Address search and suggestions from OpenStreetMap
- Address data extraction (city, state, zip code, coordinates)
- Debounced API calls
- Error handling
- Proper TypeScript types

To use this component, you would use it the same way as before:

```tsx
<OpenStreetMapAddressAutocomplete
  value={address}
  onChange={setAddress}
  onAddressSelect={handleAddressSelect}
  error={hasError}
  disabled={isDisabled}
/>
```

The main difference is that it now has a more polished UI that matches the design system you provided, while still using the OpenStreetMap API instead of Google Maps.

_**Agent**_

I've made several important changes to fix the issues:

1. Removed the debounce effect and replaced it with direct input handling:
   - Instead of using a debounce effect, we now handle input changes directly
   - This ensures immediate feedback when typing
   - We still maintain the minimum 3-character requirement before searching

2. Fixed the popover width:
   - Added `w-[var(--radix-popover-trigger-width)]` to make the popover match the trigger button width
   - Added `align="start"` to ensure proper alignment

3. Improved error handling:
   - Added `setSuggestions([])` when there's an error to clear any stale suggestions
   - Made the fetch function more robust with proper error handling

4. Optimized performance:
   - Used `useCallback` for the fetch and input handling functions to prevent unnecessary re-renders
   - Removed the unnecessary debounce effect that was causing the delay

5. Improved input handling:
   - Created a dedicated `handleInputChange` function that manages both the input state and suggestions
   - Immediately clears suggestions when input is less than 3 characters
   - Immediately fetches suggestions when input is 3 or more characters

The component should now:
- Respond immediately to input changes
- Show suggestions as soon as you type 3 or more characters
- Have a popover that matches the width of the trigger button
- Handle errors more gracefully
- Perform better with optimized callbacks

Try it now - you should see suggestions appear immediately as you type, and the popover should look much better with the matching width.

_**User (2025-06-02 20:01:06Z)**_

Still not showing anything

---

_**Agent (2025-06-02 20:01:06Z)**_

Thank you for the screenshot. If you’re seeing “No address found” for a valid address, here are the most likely causes and how to fix them:

---

### 1. **API Query Issue**
- The OpenStreetMap Nominatim API may not return results for certain queries if the query is too short, too specific, or formatted in a way it doesn’t expect.
- Try searching for a more general address (e.g., just “Celebration” or “Orlando”) to see if any results appear.

---

### 2. **API Rate Limiting or CORS**
- OpenStreetMap Nominatim has strict rate limits and may block requests if too many are made in a short time.
- Also, CORS issues can prevent results from being returned in the browser.

**Check the browser console for errors** (CORS, 429 Too Many Requests, etc.).

---

### 3. **Code Issue: Query Not Sent or Not Handled Properly**
- The fetch might not be sending the request, or the response might not be handled correctly.

---

## **Debugging Steps**

### **A. Check for API Errors in the Console**
Open your browser’s dev tools (Console and Network tabs) and:
- See if the request to `nominatim.openstreetmap.org` is being made.
- Check the response and status code.
- Look for CORS or rate limit errors.

---

### **B. Test the API Manually**
Try this in your browser:
```
https://nominatim.openstreetmap.org/search?format=json&q=80%20Celebration&addressdetails=1&limit=5
```
- If you get an empty array, the API isn’t finding that address.
- If you get results, the issue is in your code.

---

### **C. Improve the Query and Error Handling in Code**
Let’s add some logging and make the query more robust:

1. **Log the API response and errors.**
2. **Show a loading spinner when fetching.**
3. **Show a message if the API returns an empty array.**

---

## **Recommended Code Update**

Let’s add logging and a loading spinner for better debugging:

```tsx
const fetchSuggestions = useCallback(async (input: string) => {
  if (input.length < 3) {
    setSuggestions([]);
    return;
  }

  setIsLoading(true);
  try {
    const response = await fetch(
      `https://nominatim.openstreetmap.org/search?format=json&q=${encodeURIComponent(input)}&addressdetails=1&limit=5`,
      {
        headers: {
          "Accept-Language": "en-US,en",
        },
      }
    );
    const data = await response.json();
    console.log("Nominatim response:", data); // <-- Add this line
    setSuggestions(data);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Error fetching address suggestions:", error);
    setSuggestions([]);
  } finally {
    setIsLoading(false);
  }
}, []);
```

---

## **If You See CORS or Rate Limit Errors**

- For production, you may need to proxy requests through your own backend.
- For development, try using a different network or wait a few minutes.

---

## **Summary Checklist**

- [ ] Check browser console for errors (CORS, 429, etc.)
- [ ] Test the API URL directly in your browser
- [ ] Add logging to your code to see what’s returned
- [ ] Try more general queries

Would you like me to update your code to add logging and a loading spinner for easier debugging? If so, let me know!

_**User (2025-06-02 20:01:06Z)**_

When I console log it shows 5 items in an array. But its not showing anything in the list.

---

_**Agent (2025-06-02 20:01:06Z)**_

Great debugging! If the API returns 5 items but nothing shows in the list, the issue is with how the suggestions are rendered.  
This is likely due to the way the code tries to access address fields (e.g., `house_number`, `road`, etc.)—if any of these are missing, the rendered string may be empty or cause a React warning.

### **What’s likely happening:**
- The code expects every suggestion to have `address.house_number`, `address.road`, etc.
- If any of these are missing, the rendered string is blank, or React may not render the item at all.

---

## **How to Fix**

1. **Render a fallback if any address part is missing.**
2. **Show the `display_name` as a fallback.**
3. **Make sure each `CommandItem` has a unique `key` (use `suggestion.place_id` or `suggestion.osm_id`).**

---

## **Edit the Rendering Code**

Replace this part:
```tsx
{suggestion.address.house_number} {suggestion.address.road}, {suggestion.address.town ? suggestion.address.town : suggestion.address.county}, {suggestion.address.state} {suggestion.address.postcode}
```
with:
```tsx
{suggestion.display_name || "Unknown address"}
```
Or, for a more robust display:
```tsx
{[
  suggestion.address?.house_number,
  suggestion.address?.road,
  suggestion.address?.town || suggestion.address?.county,
  suggestion.address?.state,
  suggestion.address?.postcode
].filter(Boolean).join(", ") || suggestion.display_name}
```

And for the key, use:
```tsx
key={suggestion.place_id || suggestion.osm_id || index}
```

---

## **Full Example for the List Rendering**

```tsx
<CommandGroup heading="Suggestions">
  {suggestions.map((suggestion, index) => (
    <CommandItem
      key={suggestion.place_id || suggestion.osm_id || index}
      value={suggestion.place_id}
      onSelect={() => handleSelect(suggestion)}
    >
      <MapPin className="mr-2 h-4 w-4" />
      {[
        suggestion.address?.house_number,
        suggestion.address?.road,
        suggestion.address?.town || suggestion.address?.county,
        suggestion.address?.state,
        suggestion.address?.postcode
      ].filter(Boolean).join(", ") || suggestion.display_name}
      <Check
        className={cn(
          "ml-auto h-4 w-4",
          value === suggestion.display_name ? "opacity-100" : "opacity-0"
        )}
      />
    </CommandItem>
  ))}
</CommandGroup>
```

---

_**Agent (2025-06-02 20:16:39Z)**_

Absolutely! Here’s a robust, production-ready React email template for inviting users to their masjid. It uses props for variables, is styled inline for maximum compatibility (Gmail, Outlook, etc.), and is easy to customize.  
You can use this with libraries like [Resend](https://resend.com/docs/send-emails/emails-with-react) or [React Email](https://react.email/).

---

```tsx
// components/emails/InvitingUser.tsx

import * as React from "react";

interface InvitingUserProps {
  userName: string;
  masjidName: string;
  inviteLink: string;
  inviterName?: string;
  supportEmail?: string;
}

export function InvitingUser({
  userName,
  masjidName,
  inviteLink,
  inviterName = "A member of your community",
  supportEmail = "support@mizan.com",
}: InvitingUserProps) {
  return (
    <html>
      <body
        style={{
          background: "#f9f6f4",
          fontFamily: "Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif",
          margin: 0,
          padding: 0,
        }}
      >
        <table
          width="100%"
          cellPadding={0}
          cellSpacing={0}
          style={{ background: "#f9f6f4", minHeight: "100vh" }}
        >
          <tr>
            <td align="center">
              <table
                width="100%"
                max-width="600"
                style={{
                  background: "#fff",
                  borderRadius: "8px",
                  boxShadow: "0 2px 8px rgba(85,12,24,0.08)",
                  margin: "40px 0",
                  padding: "32px",
                  border: "1px solid #f3e7e7",
                  maxWidth: "600px",
                }}
              >
                <tr>
                  <td align="center" style={{ paddingBottom: "24px" }}>
                    <img
                      src="https://mizan-static.s3.amazonaws.com/logo-mizan.png"
                      alt="Mizan Logo"
                      width="80"
                      style={{ display: "block" }}
                    />
                  </td>
                </tr>
                <tr>
                  <td>
                    <h2
                      style={{
                        color: "#550C18",
                        fontWeight: 700,
                        margin: "0 0 16px 0",
                        fontSize: "24px",
                        letterSpacing: "0.5px",
                      }}
                    >
                      You're Invited to {masjidName}!
                    </h2>
                    <p style={{ color: "#222", fontSize: "16px", margin: "0 0 16px 0" }}>
                      Salaam {userName},
                    </p>
                    <p style={{ color: "#222", fontSize: "16px", margin: "0 0 24px 0" }}>
                      {inviterName} has invited you to join <b>{masjidName}</b> on Mizan.
                      Click the button below to accept your invitation and get started!
                    </p>
                    <div style={{ textAlign: "center", margin: "32px 0" }}>
                      <a
                        href={inviteLink}
                        style={{
                          background: "#550C18",
                          color: "#fff",
                          padding: "14px 32px",
                          borderRadius: "6px",
                          textDecoration: "none",
                          fontWeight: 600,
                          fontSize: "16px",
                          letterSpacing: "0.5px",
                          display: "inline-block",
                        }}
                        target="_blank"
                        rel="noopener noreferrer"
                      >
                        Accept Invitation
                      </a>
                    </div>
                    <p style={{ color: "#666", fontSize: "14px", margin: "0 0 8px 0" }}>
                      If you have any questions, reply to this email or contact us at{" "}
                      <a href={`mailto:${supportEmail}`} style={{ color: "#550C18" }}>
                        {supportEmail}
                      </a>
                      .
                    </p>
                    <p style={{ color: "#bbb", fontSize: "12px", margin: "32px 0 0 0" }}>
                      If you did not expect this invitation, you can safely ignore this email.
                    </p>
                  </td>
                </tr>
              </table>
              <table width="100%" style={{ marginTop: "16px" }}>
                <tr>
                  <td align="center" style={{ color: "#bbb", fontSize: "12px" }}>
                    &copy; {new Date().getFullYear()} Mizan. All rights reserved.
                  </td>
                </tr>
              </table>
            </td>
          </tr>
        </table>
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}

export default InvitingUser;
```

---

**How to use:

_**Agent (2025-06-02 20:16:39Z)**_

Perfect! Resend works great with React email templates like the one I provided. Here are a few tips and a quick usage example to ensure your template works perfectly with Resend:

---

## 1. **File Structure & Export**
- Make sure your component is a default export (which it is in the template above).
- The file should be in your project (e.g., `components/emails/InvitingUser.tsx`).

---

## 2. **How to Use with Resend**

Here’s an example of how to send the invitation email using Resend in your API route or server function:

```ts
import { Resend } from 'resend';
import InvitingUser from '@/components/emails/InvitingUser';

const resend = new Resend(process.env.RESEND_API_KEY);

export async function sendMasjidInvite({
  to,
  userName,
  masjidName,
  inviteLink,
  inviterName,
  supportEmail,
}: {
  to: string;
  userName: string;
  masjidName: string;
  inviteLink: string;
  inviterName?: string;
  supportEmail?: string;
}) {
  await resend.emails.send({
    from: 'Mizan <noreply@mizan.com>',
    to,
    subject: `You're invited to ${masjidName} on Mizan!`,
    react: (
      <InvitingUser
        userName={userName}
        masjidName={masjidName}
        inviteLink={inviteLink}
        inviterName={inviterName}
        supportEmail={supportEmail}
      />
    ),
  });
}
```

---

## 3. **Styling & Compatibility**
- All styles are inline for maximum compatibility (Gmail, Outlook, etc.).
- Images should be absolute URLs (as in the template).
- Avoid using custom fonts or external CSS.

---

## 4. **Testing**
- Use [Resend’s dashboard](https://resend.com/dashboard) or your own test email to preview the result.
- You can also use [React Email’s preview](https://react.email/docs/preview-email) during development.

---

**You’re all set!**  
If you want any tweaks to the template (branding, more info, etc.), just let me know!

@schema.prisma

_**Agent (2025-06-02 20:32:12Z)**_

You have a **Prisma relation error** because your relation fields are not consistently named and are missing the correct `@relation` attributes on both sides of the relation.

### **What's wrong?**
- In `Masjid`, you have:  
  ```prisma
  masjidInvites MasjidInvite[]
  ```
  but in `MasjidInvite`, you have:
  ```prisma
  masjid Masjid @relation("MasjidInviteToMasjid", fields: [masjidId], references: [id])
  ```
- In `User`, you have:
  ```prisma
  MasjidInvites MasjidInvite[] @relation("MasjidInviteToMasjid")
  ```
  but in `MasjidInvite`, you do not have a matching relation for this.

### **How to fix:**
You need to make sure that both sides of the relation have the same `@relation` name and that the array side and the single side are matched.

---

## **Corrected Schema Snippet**

Replace your current `Masjid`, `User`, and `MasjidInvite` relation fields with the following:

```prisma
model Masjid {
  // ... other fields ...
  masjidInvites MasjidInvite[] @relation("MasjidInviteToMasjid")
  // ... other fields ...
}

model User {
  // ... other fields ...
  masjidInvites MasjidInvite[] @relation("MasjidInviteToUser")
  masjidInvitedTo MasjidInvite[] @relation("MasjidInvitedToUser")
  // ... other fields ...
}

model MasjidInvite {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  masjidId    String
  masjid      Masjid   @relation("MasjidInviteToMasjid", fields: [masjidId], references: [id])
  userId      String
  user        User     @relation("MasjidInviteToUser", fields: [userId], references: [id])
  invitedById String
  invitedBy   User     @relation("MasjidInvitedToUser", fields: [invitedById], references: [id])
  status      String   @default("pending")
  token       String
  expiresAt   DateTime
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt
}
```

**Key points:**
- The relation name must match on both sides.
- The array side is on the parent (`Masjid`, `User`), the single side is on the child (`MasjidInvite`).
- You should not have multiple fields with the same relation name in the same model.

---

## **Summary Table**

| Model         | Field Name         | Relation Name              | Type                |
|---------------|--------------------|----------------------------|---------------------|
| Masjid        | masjidInvites      | MasjidInviteToMasjid       | MasjidInvite[]      |
| MasjidInvite  | masjid             | MasjidInviteToMasjid       | Masjid              |
| User          | masjidInvites      | MasjidInviteToUser         | MasjidInvite[]      |
| MasjidInvite  | user               | MasjidInviteToUser         | User                |
| User          | masjidInvitedTo    | MasjidInvitedToUser        | MasjidInvite[]      |
| MasjidInvite  | invitedBy          | MasjidInvitedToUser        | User                |

---

## **What to do next**
1. Update your `schema.prisma` as above.
2. Run:
   ```sh
   npx prisma format
   npx prisma generate
   ```
3. If you have existing migrations, you may need to create a new migration:
   ```sh
   npx prisma migrate dev --name fix-masjid-invite-relations
   ```

---

Let me know if you want me to apply the exact edit to your `schema.prisma` file!

_**Agent (2025-06-03 21:43:26Z)**_

You are seeing these errors because **Prisma does not support `onDelete` or `onUpdate` on the array side of a relation** (the side with `[]`).  
You should only specify `onDelete` on the single-object side (the side with `fields: [...]` and `references: [...]`).

**Additionally, you cannot use `onDelete` on implicit many-to-many relations** (like `users User[] @relation("MasjidUsers")`).

---

## **How to Fix**

### 1. **Remove `onDelete` from array sides and implicit many-to-many relations**

- For example, change:
  ```prisma
  masjidInvites MasjidInvite[] @relation("MasjidInviteToMasjid", onDelete: Cascade)
  ```
  to:
  ```prisma
  masjidInvites MasjidInvite[] @relation("MasjidInviteToMasjid")
  ```

- And change:
  ```prisma
  users User[] @relation("MasjidUsers", onDelete: Cascade)
  ```
  to:
  ```prisma
  users User[] @relation("MasjidUsers")
  ```

- And change:
  ```prisma
  masjids Masjid[] @relation("MasjidUsers", onDelete: Cascade)
  ```
  to:
  ```prisma
  masjids Masjid[] @relation("MasjidUsers")
  ```

- And change:
  ```prisma
  masjidInvites MasjidInvite[] @relation("MasjidInviteToUser", onDelete: Cascade)
  masjidInvitedTo MasjidInvite[] @relation("MasjidInvitedToUser", onDelete: Cascade)
  ```
  to:
  ```prisma
  masjidInvites MasjidInvite[] @relation("MasjidInviteToUser")
  masjidInvitedTo MasjidInvite[] @relation("MasjidInvitedToUser")
  ```

- And change:
  ```prisma
  displayedContent  Content[] @relation("DisplayContent", onDelete: Cascade)
  ```
  to:
  ```prisma
  displayedContent  Content[] @relation("DisplayContent")
  ```

### 2. **Keep `onDelete` only on the single-object side**
For example, this is correct:
```prisma
masjid   Masjid @relation(fields: [masjidId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
```

---

## **Summary Table**

| Model/Field (array side)         | Remove `onDelete`? |  
|----------------------------------|--------------------|
| users User[] @relation("MasjidUsers", onDelete: Cascade) | YES |
| masjids Masjid[] @relation("MasjidUsers", onDelete: Cascade) | YES |
| masjidInvites MasjidInvite[] @relation("MasjidInviteToMasjid", onDelete: Cascade) | YES |
| masjidInvites MasjidInvite[] @relation("MasjidInviteToUser", onDelete: Cascade) | YES |
| masjidInvitedTo MasjidInvite[] @relation("MasjidInvitedToUser", onDelete: Cascade) | YES |
| displayedContent Content[] @relation("DisplayContent", onDelete: Cascade) | YES |

---

## **Would you like me to apply these fixes to your schema?**
I can do it automatically for you!

    Syntax Error

./components/DashboardSidebar.tsx

./app/dashboard/layout.tsx

---

_**Agent (2025-06-03 22:08:02Z)**_

This error usually means that your build system (or a linter/parser) is not recognizing `<SidebarProvider>` as a valid JSX component. The most common causes are:

1. **You are using `"use client";` at the top, but one of the imported components is not a client component.**
2. **You are using a custom JSX pragma or a non-standard JSX parser.**
3. **You have a typo or a missing export in your `@/components/ui/sidebar` file.**
4. **Your build system is misconfigured or caching an old version.**

---

## **How to Fix**

### 1. **Check the Export in `@/components/ui/sidebar`**
Make sure `SidebarProvider` is exported as a React component from `@/components/ui/sidebar`.  
It should look like:
```tsx
export function SidebarProvider({ children, ...props }) {
  // ...
  return <SidebarContext.Provider value={...}>{children}</SidebarContext.Provider>;
}
```
or
```tsx
export const SidebarProvider = ({ children, ...props }) => { ... }
```

If it's a default export, you should import it as:
```tsx
import SidebarProvider from "@/components/ui/sidebar";
```
If it's a named export, you should import it as:
```tsx
import { SidebarProvider } from "@/components/ui/sidebar";
```

---

### 2. **Check for Typos**
Make sure you are not accidentally importing a non-component or a non-existent export.

---

### 3. **Check Your JSX Syntax**
Make sure you are not using a reserved word or a non-component as a JSX tag.

---

### 4. **Restart Your Dev Server**
Sometimes, Next.js or your bundler caches an old version.  
Stop and restart your dev server:
```sh
npm run dev
# or
yarn dev
```

---

### 5. **Check for Mixed Client/Server Components**
If any of the sidebar components are server components, you cannot use them inside a `"use client"` file.

---

## **Summary Table**

| Problem | Solution |
|---------|----------|
| SidebarProvider not exported | Export it as a React component |
| Wrong import style | Use named or default import as appropriate |
| Caching/build issue | Restart dev server |
| Mixed client/server | Ensure all sidebar components are client components |

---

**Would you like me to check your `@/components/ui/sidebar` file for the correct export?**  
If so, let me know!

---

_**Agent (2025-06-03 23:26:14Z)**_

The error is:
```
IntegrationError: Invalid value for elements.create('address'): defaultValues.address.state should be a string. You specified: {}.
```
This means that the `state` field in the `defaultValues.address` object passed to `<AddressElement />` is **not a string** (it's `{}` or possibly `undefined`).

---

## **Why?**
Your code:
```tsx
state: getStateAbbreviation(shippingData?.state!) ? getStateAbbreviation(shippingData?.state!) : shippingData?.state,
```
- If `getStateAbbreviation` returns a **Promise** (async function), or if `shippingData?.state` is `undefined`, this will not be a string.
- The Stripe AddressElement expects a plain string for `state`.

---

## **How to Fix**

### 1. **Ensure `getStateAbbreviation` is synchronous and returns a string**
If it's async, you must not call it here.  
If it's synchronous, make sure it always returns a string or `undefined`.

### 2. **Guard against undefined values**
Only include the `state` property if it is a string.

### 3. **Fix the code block**
Replace:
```tsx
state: getStateAbbreviation(shippingData?.state!) ? getStateAbbreviation(shippingData?.state!) : shippingData?.state,
```
with:
```tsx
state: typeof shippingData?.state === 'string' ? getStateAbbreviation(shippingData.state) || shippingData.state : undefined,
```
**But only if `getStateAbbreviation` is synchronous!**

If you want to be extra safe, you can do:
```tsx
state: typeof shippingData?.state === 'string'
  ? (getStateAbbreviation(shippingData.state) || shippingData.state)
  : undefined,
```

Or, if you want to only include the property if it is a string:
```tsx
address: {
  line1: shippingData?.address || "",
  city: shippingData?.city || "",
  state: typeof shippingData?.state === 'string'
    ? (getStateAbbreviation(shippingData.state) || shippingData.state)
    : "",
  postal_code: shippingData?.zipCode || "",
  country: shippingData?.country || "",
},
```

---

## **Summary Table**

| Problem | Solution |
|---------|----------|
| `state` is not a string | Ensure it is always a string or `undefined` |
| `getStateAbbreviation` is async | Do not use it here, or make it sync |

---

**Would you like me to apply this fix to your file?**  
Let me know if `getStateAbbreviation` is async or sync, and I can make the edit for you!

_**Agent (2025-06-04 00:14:13Z)**_

You are seeing this error:
```
PrismaClient is unable to run in this browser environment, or has been bundled for the browser (running in ``).
```
because **Prisma Client can only be used in Node.js server environments, not in the browser or in code that runs on the client side**.

---

## **Why does this happen?**
- You are likely importing or using `prisma` (the Prisma Client) in code that is executed on the client (browser), such as in a React component, a Next.js page with `"use client"`, or in middleware that runs on the edge or client.
- Prisma is meant for server-side use only (API routes, getServerSideProps, server actions, etc).

---

## **How to Fix**

### 1. **Only use Prisma in server-side code**
- Move all Prisma code to API routes, server actions, or server components.
- Never import or use Prisma in files/components that run on the client.

### 2. **For Next.js Middleware**
- You cannot use Prisma in Next.js middleware (middleware runs on the edge, not in Node.js).
- If you need to check database health or log events, do it in an API route or server action, not in middleware.

### 3. **For Logging**
- If you want to log events from the client, send a request to an API route or server action, and do the Prisma logging there.

---

## **Example Fix**

**Before (incorrect, in middleware or client):**
```ts
import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma";

export async function logEvent(event) {
  await prisma.systemLog.create({ data: event });
}
```

**After (correct, in API route or server action):**
```ts
// In /app/api/log-event/route.ts
import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma";
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";

export async function POST(req) {
  const event = await req.json();
  await prisma.systemLog.create({ data: event });
  return NextResponse.json({ success: true });
}
```
Then, from the client, call this API route.

---

## **Summary Table**

| Where?         | Can use Prisma? |
|----------------|----------------|
| API Route      | ✅ Yes         |
| Server Action  | ✅ Yes         |
| Server Component | ✅ Yes      |
| Middleware     | ❌ No          |
| Client Component | ❌ No        |

---

**If you want, I can help you refactor your logging or database code to be server-only. Just tell me where you want to use it!**

---

_**Agent**_

Your `lib/logger.ts` is already marked with `"use server"`, which is correct for server-only code.  
**However, the error occurs if you import or use any of these logging functions in client-side or middleware code.**

---

## **How to Refactor and Fix**

### 1. **Keep `lib/logger.ts` as server-only**
- Do not import or use any function from `lib/logger.ts` in client components or middleware.

### 2. **If you need to log from the client or middleware:**
- Create an API route (e.g. `/api/log-event`) that calls your logger.
- From the client or middleware, send a `fetch` request to this API route.

---

## **Example: API Route for Logging**

**Create `app/api/log-event/route.ts`:**
```ts
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
import { logEvent } from "@/lib/logger";

export async function POST(req: Request) {
  const body = await req.json();
  await logEvent(body);
  return NextResponse.json({ success: true });
}
```

---

## **Example: Client/Middleware Usage**

**From the client or middleware, use:**
```ts
await fetch("/api/log-event", {
  method: "POST",
  headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
  body: JSON.stringify({
    action: "login",
    entityType: "user",
    entityId: "123",
    userId: "123",
    details: "User logged in",
  }),
});
```

---

## **Summary Table**

| Where?         | How to log?                |
|----------------|---------------------------|
| Server code    | Use `logEvent` directly   |
| API route      | Use `logEvent` directly   |
| Client code    | Call `/api/log-event`     |
| Middleware     | Call `/api/log-event`     |

---

**Would you like me to create the API route for you?**  
If yes, I’ll add the file and show you how to use it!

---

_**Agent (2025-06-04 00:14:13Z)**_

Absolutely, I can help!  
You are getting this error because you are trying to use Prisma (which is server-only) in code that runs in the browser or in Next.js middleware. **Prisma cannot be used in middleware or client-side code.**

---

## **How to Fix This**

### 1. **Do NOT use Prisma in middleware or client code.**
- Only use Prisma in API routes, server actions, or server components.

### 2. **If you need to log from middleware, use an API route.**

#### **Step-by-step solution:**

---

### **A. Create an API Route for Logging**

Create a file at:  
`app/api/log-event/route.ts`

```ts
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
import { logEvent } from "@/lib/logger";

export async function POST(req: Request) {
  const body = await req.json();
  await logEvent(body);
  return NextResponse.json({ success: true });
}
```

---

### **B. In Your Middleware, Call the API Route Instead of Using Prisma Directly**

**Instead of:**
```ts
import { logSystemEvent } from "@/lib/logger";

await logSystemEvent(...); // ❌ This will break in middleware!
```

**Do:**
```ts
await fetch(`${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL || "http://localhost:3000"}/api/log-event`, {
  method: "POST",
  headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
  body: JSON.stringify({
    action: "your_action",
    entityType: "your_entity",
    entityId: "your_id",
    userId: "system",
    details: "details here",
  }),
});
```
- You may need to set `NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL` in your `.env` for production.

---

### **C. Remove All Direct Prisma Usage from Middleware**

**Do not import or use anything from `@/lib/db` or `@/lib/logger` in your middleware.**

---

## **Summary Table**

| Where?         | How to log?                |
|----------------|---------------------------|
| API route      | Use `logEvent` directly   |
| Server action  | Use `logEvent` directly   |
| Middleware     | Call `/api/log-event`     |
| Client         | Call `/api/log-event`     |

---

**If you want, I can create the API route file for you and show you exactly what to change in your middleware.  
Would you like me to do that? If yes, please tell me the path to your middleware file!**

_**User (2025-06-04 00:14:13Z)**_

Why is it when I login my auth session is not setting. it still shows null for session

---

_**Agent (2025-06-04 00:14:13Z)**_

When your auth session is not being set (i.e., `session` is always `null` after login), it’s usually due to one of these common issues in Next.js/NextAuth or custom auth setups:

---

## **Common Causes & Fixes**

### 1. **Middleware Interfering with Auth Cookies**
- If your `middleware.ts` is matching `/api/:path*`, it will run on all API routes—including your auth routes (e.g., `/api/auth/*` for NextAuth).
- If your middleware returns a response or mutates headers/cookies, it can break session handling.

**Fix:**  
**Exclude your auth routes from the middleware matcher.**  
Change:
```js
export const config = {
  matcher: "/api/:path*",
}
```
to:
```js
export const config = {
  matcher: ["/api/:path*", "!/api/auth/:path*"],
}
```
This will prevent the middleware from running on `/api/auth/*` routes.

---

### 2. **Session Cookie Not Being Set**
- Check your browser’s dev tools → Application → Cookies. Is the session cookie being set after login?
- If not, check your auth provider’s callback URL, domain, and cookie settings.

---

### 3. **Secure/HttpOnly Cookie Issues**
- If you’re on `localhost` and using `secure: true` for cookies, they won’t be set unless you’re on HTTPS.
- Make sure your cookie config is correct for your environment.

---

### 4. **API Route or Server Action Not Returning Session**
- If you’re using NextAuth, make sure you’re calling `getServerSession` or `getSession` correctly in your API routes or server components.

---

### 5. **Frontend Not Refreshing Session**
- After login, you may need to refresh the page or call `getSession()` again to get the updated session.

---

## **What to Do**

1. **Update your middleware config to exclude `/api/auth/*`:**
   ```js
   export const config = {
     matcher: ["/api/:path*", "!/api/auth/:path*"],
   }
   ```

2. **Check your cookies in the browser.**
   - If no session cookie is set, check your auth provider and cookie settings.

3. **If using NextAuth, check your `[...nextauth].ts` config for correct URLs and cookie settings.**

4. **Restart your dev server after making changes.**

---

## **Summary Table**

| Problem                        | Solution                                      |
|---------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| Middleware intercepts auth      | Exclude `/api/auth/*` from matcher            |
| Cookie not set                  | Check cookie config, domain, secure flag      |
| Session not refreshed           | Call `getSession()` or refresh page           |
| Wrong callback URLs             | Check NextAuth/provider config                |

---

**Try the middleware matcher fix first—it’s the most common cause!**  
Let me know if you want me to update your `middleware.ts` for you.

---

        "originalContents": "\n\n// Get all orders (admin)\nexport const getAllOrders = async () =\u003e {\n  const o = await prisma.orders.findMany({\n    include: {\n      user: true\n    },\n    orderBy: {\n      createdAt: 'desc'\n    }\n  });\n  return o;\n};",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\"use server\";\n\nimport { prisma } from \"../db\";\nimport { stripeClient } from \"../stripe\";\n\nexport const getSessionAndOrder = async (sessionId: string) =\u003e {\n  const checkoutSession = await prisma.checkoutSessions.findFirst({\n    where: {\n      sessionId\n    },\n    include: {\n      user: true\n    }\n  });\n  const order = await prisma.orders.findFirst({\n    where: {\n      stripeSessionId: sessionId\n    }\n  });\n  if (!checkoutSession) {\n    return {\n      order: null,\n      checkoutSession: null,\n      stripeSession: null\n    };\n  }\n  const stripeSession = await stripeClient.checkout.sessions.retrieve(checkoutSession?.sessionId as string);\n\n  return {\n    order,\n    checkoutSession,\n    stripeSession: stripeSession ? {\n      amount_total: stripeSession.amount_total,\n      created: stripeSession.created\n    } : null\n  };\n}",

        "originalContents": "\"use server\";\n\nimport { prisma } from \"../db\";\nimport { stripeClient } from \"../stripe\";\n\nexport const getSessionAndOrder = async (sessionId: string) =\u003e {\n  const checkoutSession = await prisma.checkoutSessions.findFirst({\n    where: {\n      sessionId\n    },\n    include: {\n      user: true\n    }\n  });\n  const order = await prisma.orders.findFirst({\n    where: {\n      stripeSessionId: sessionId\n    }\n  });\n  if (!checkoutSession) {\n    return {\n      order: null,\n      checkoutSession: null,\n      stripeSession: null\n    };\n  }\n  const stripeSession = await stripeClient.checkout.sessions.retrieve(checkoutSession?.sessionId as string);\n\n  return {\n    order,\n    checkoutSession,\n    stripeSession: stripeSession ? {\n      amount_total: stripeSession.amount_total,\n      created: stripeSession.created\n    } : null\n  };\n}",

        "contents": "\n\nexport const getPaymentAndOrder = async (sessionId: string) =\u003e {\n  const checkoutSession = await prisma.checkoutSessions.findFirst({\n    where: {\n      sessionId\n    },\n    include: {\n      user: true\n    }\n  });\n  const order = await prisma.orders.findFirst({\n    where: {\n      stripeSessionId: sessionId\n    }\n  });\n\n  const stripeSession = await stripeClient.paymentIntents.retrieve(checkoutSession?.sessionId as string);\n\n  return {\n    order,\n    checkoutSession,\n    stripeSession: stripeSession ? {\n      amount_total: stripeSession.amount,\n      created: stripeSession.created\n    } : null\n  };\n}",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\"use server\";\nimport { prisma } from \"@/lib/db\";\n\nexport const getAllAnnouncements = async (masjidId?: string) =\u003e {\n  return prisma.announcement.findMany({\n    where: masjidId ? { masjidId } : undefined,\n    include: { displays: true, contentItem: true },\n    orderBy: { createdAt: \"desc\" },\n  });\n};",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\nexport const getRecentDonations = async (masjidId?: string, page = 1, pageSize = 10) =\u003e {\n  const where: any = { status: \"completed\" };\n  if (masjidId) where.masjidId = masjidId;\n  const [donations, total] = await Promise.all([\n    prisma.donation.findMany({\n      where,\n      orderBy: { createdAt: \"desc\" },\n      skip: (page - 1) * pageSize,\n      take: pageSize,\n      include: {\n        category: true,\n      },\n    }),\n    prisma.donation.count({ where }),\n  ]);\n  return { donations, total };\n};",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 78
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 79
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80,
            "text": "export const getRecentDonations = async (masjidId?: string, page = 1, pageSize = 10) =\u003e {"

          },

            "lineNumber": 82,

            "lineNumber": 88,

        "originalContents": "\n\nexport const getRecentDonations = async (masjidId?: string, page = 1, pageSize = 10) =\u003e {\n  const where: any = { status: \"completed\" };\n  if (masjidId) where.masjidId = masjidId;\n  const [donations, total] = await Promise.all([\n    prisma.donation.findMany({\n      where,\n      orderBy: { createdAt: \"desc\" },\n      skip: (page - 1) * pageSize,\n      take: pageSize,\n      include: {\n        category: true,\n      },\n    }),\n    prisma.donation.count({ where }),\n  ]);\n  return { donations, total };\n};",

        "originalContents": "\n\nexport const getAnnouncementById = async (id: string) =\u003e {\n  return prisma.announcement.findUnique({\n    where: { id },\n    include: { displays: true, contentItem: true },\n  });\n};",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\nexport async function createPaymentIntent({ amount, cart, discount, shippingData }: CreatePaymentIntentParams) {\n  const user = await getUser();\n  if(!user) return {\n    error: true,\n    message: \"Please login\"\n  };\n  try {\n    // Create a PaymentIntent with the order amount and currency\n    const paymentIntent = await stripeClient.paymentIntents.create({\n      amount: Math.round(amount * 100), // Convert to cents\n      currency: \"usd\",\n      automatic_payment_methods: {\n        enabled: true,\n      },\n      customer: user.stripeCustomerId || \"\",\n      metadata: {\n        cart_items: JSON.stringify(\n          cart.map((item) =\u003e ({\n            id: item.productId,\n            name: item.name,\n            quantity: item.quantity,\n            price: item.price,\n          })),\n        ),\n        discount_code: discount?.code || \"\",\n        discount_percent: discount?.percent?.toString() || \"0\",\n      },\n    });\n\n    const dbIntent = await prisma.checkoutSessions.create({\n      data: {\n        id: v4(),\n        cart: JSON.stringify(cart),\n        completed: \"pending\",\n        paymentType: 'payment',\n        sessionId: paymentIntent.id,\n        userId: user.id,\n        shippingData: shippingData\n      }\n    });\n\n\n    return {\n      clientSecret: paymentIntent.client_secret,\n      id: paymentIntent.id\n    }\n  } catch (error) {\n    console.error(\"Error creating payment intent:\", error)\n    throw new Error(\"Failed to create payment intent\")\n  }\n}",

          },

        "originalContents": "\nexport async function createPaymentIntent({ amount, cart, discount, shippingData }: CreatePaymentIntentParams) {\n  const user = await getUser();\n  if(!user) return {\n    error: true,\n    message: \"Please login\"\n  };\n  try {\n    // Create a PaymentIntent with the order amount and currency\n    const paymentIntent = await stripeClient.paymentIntents.create({\n      amount: Math.round(amount * 100), // Convert to cents\n      currency: \"usd\",\n      automatic_payment_methods: {\n        enabled: true,\n      },\n      customer: user.stripeCustomerId || \"\",\n      metadata: {\n        cart_items: JSON.stringify(\n          cart.map((item) =\u003e ({\n            id: item.productId,\n            name: item.name,\n            quantity: item.quantity,\n            price: item.price,\n          })),\n        ),\n        discount_code: discount?.code || \"\",\n        discount_percent: discount?.percent?.toString() || \"0\",\n      },\n    });\n\n    const dbIntent = await prisma.checkoutSessions.create({\n      data: {\n        id: v4(),\n        cart: JSON.stringify(cart),\n        completed: \"pending\",\n        paymentType: 'payment',\n        sessionId: paymentIntent.id,\n        userId: user.id,\n        shippingData: shippingData\n      }\n    });\n\n\n    return {\n      clientSecret: paymentIntent.client_secret,\n      id: paymentIntent.id\n    }\n  } catch (error) {\n    console.error(\"Error creating payment intent:\", error)\n    throw new Error(\"Failed to create payment intent\")\n  }\n}",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\nexport const getDonationOverview = async (masjidId?: string) =\u003e {\n  const now = new Date();\n  const start = startOfMonth(now);\n  const end = endOfMonth(now);\n  const where: any = {\n    createdAt: { gte: start, lte: end },\n    status: \"completed\",\n  };\n  if (masjidId) where.masjidId = masjidId;\n  const grouped = await prisma.donation.groupBy({\n    by: [\"categoryId\"],\n    where,\n    _sum: { amount: true },\n  });\n  const categories = await prisma.donationCategory.findMany({\n    where: { id: { in: grouped.map(g =\u003e g.categoryId) } },\n  });\n  return grouped.map(g =\u003e ({\n    ...g,\n    category: categories.find(c =\u003e c.id === g.categoryId),\n  }));\n};",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 55
          },

            "lineNumber": 56

        "originalContents": "\n\nexport const getDonationOverview = async (masjidId?: string) =\u003e {\n  const now = new Date();\n  const start = startOfMonth(now);\n  const end = endOfMonth(now);\n  const where: any = {\n    createdAt: { gte: start, lte: end },\n    status: \"completed\",\n  };\n  if (masjidId) where.masjidId = masjidId;\n  const grouped = await prisma.donation.groupBy({\n    by: [\"categoryId\"],\n    where,\n    _sum: { amount: true },\n  });\n  const categories = await prisma.donationCategory.findMany({\n    where: { id: { in: grouped.map(g =\u003e g.categoryId) } },\n  });\n  return grouped.map(g =\u003e ({\n    ...g,\n    category: categories.find(c =\u003e c.id === g.categoryId),\n  }));\n};",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\"use server\";\n\nimport { Product } from \"@prisma/client\";\nimport { prisma } from \"../db\";\n\n// Function to get all products\nexport async function getProducts() {\n  const products = await prisma.product.findMany();\n\n  return products || [];;\n}",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\"use server\";\nimport { v4 } from \"uuid\";\nimport { CartItem } from \"../cartItemSchema\";\nimport { prisma } from \"../db\";\nimport { stripeClient } from \"../stripe\";\nimport { Discount } from \"../useCartStore\";\nimport { getUser } from \"./user\";\n\ntype CreateCheckoutSessionProps = {\n  cart: CartItem[];\n  discount: Discount | null;\n  card: {\n    number: number,\n    cvv: number,\n    name: string\n  };\n}",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "\"use server\";"

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\nexport const getTopDonationCategories = async (masjidId?: string) =\u003e {\n  const now = new Date();\n  const start = startOfMonth(now);\n  const end = endOfMonth(now);\n  const where: any = {\n    createdAt: { gte: start, lte: end },\n    status: \"completed\",\n  };\n  if (masjidId) where.masjidId = masjidId;\n  const top = await prisma.donation.groupBy({\n    by: [\"categoryId\"],\n    where,\n    _sum: { amount: true },\n    orderBy: { _sum: { amount: \"desc\" } },\n    take: 3,\n  });\n  const categories = await prisma.donationCategory.findMany({\n    where: { id: { in: top.map(t =\u003e t.categoryId) } },\n  });\n  return top.map(t =\u003e ({\n    ...t,\n    category: categories.find(c =\u003e c.id === t.categoryId),\n  }));\n};",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },

            "lineNumber": 55,

        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\nexport const getTopDonationCategories = async (masjidId?: string) =\u003e {\n  const now = new Date();\n  const start = startOfMonth(now);\n  const end = endOfMonth(now);\n  const where: any = {\n    createdAt: { gte: start, lte: end },\n    status: \"completed\",\n  };\n  if (masjidId) where.masjidId = masjidId;\n  const top = await prisma.donation.groupBy({\n    by: [\"categoryId\"],\n    where,\n    _sum: { amount: true },\n    orderBy: { _sum: { amount: \"desc\" } },\n    take: 3,\n  });\n  const categories = await prisma.donationCategory.findMany({\n    where: { id: { in: top.map(t =\u003e t.categoryId) } },\n  });\n  return top.map(t =\u003e ({\n    ...t,\n    category: categories.find(c =\u003e c.id === t.categoryId),\n  }));\n};",
        "range": {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\nexport const createCheckoutPage = async ({\n  cart,\n  discount,\n}: CreateCheckoutPageProps) =\u003e {\n  const user = await getUser();\n  if(!user) return {\n    error: true,\n    message: \"Please login\"\n  };\n  const products = [];\n  const line_items = [];\n\n  for (const product of cart) {\n    const pro = await prisma.product.findFirst({\n      where: {\n        id: product.productId\n      }\n    });\n    if(pro) {\n      console.log(pro);\n      products.push(pro);\n      line_items.push({\n        price_data: {\n          currency: \"USD\",\n          product_data: {\n            name: pro.name,\n            images: [pro.image],\n          },\n          unit_amount: Math.round(Number(product.price) * 100),\n        },\n        quantity: 1,\n      })\n    }\n  };\n\n  if(products.find((e) =\u003e e?.requiredSubscriptionId \u0026\u0026 e?.requiredSubscriptionId.length \u003c 1) \u0026\u0026 products.length \u003e 1) return {\n    error: true,\n    message: \"When purchasing a subscription please only have the subscription in your cart. Jazakallahu Khair\"\n  };\n\n  const subscription = products.find((e) =\u003e e?.requiredSubscriptionId \u0026\u0026 e?.requiredSubscriptionId.length \u003e 1) || null ;\n\n  if(!subscription) return {\n    redirect: true\n  };\n\n  // @ts-ignore Ignore\n  const session = await stripeClient.checkout.sessions.create({\n    payment_method_types: ['card'],\n    line_items: subscription ? [{\n      price: subscription?.requiredSubscriptionId,\n      quantity: 1\n    }] : line_items,\n    mode: subscription ? \"subscription\" : 'payment', // or 'payment' for one-time purchases\n    success_url: &#96;${process.env.DOMAIN}/order-confirmation?session_id={CHECKOUT_SESSION_ID}&#96;,\n    cancel_url: &#96;${process.env.DOMAIN}/cart&#96;,\n    customer: user.stripeCustomerId || null,\n    shipping_address_collection: {\n      allowed_countries: [\n        \"US\"\n      ]\n    },\n  });",

        "originalContents": "\n\nexport const createCheckoutPage = async ({\n  cart,\n  discount,\n}: CreateCheckoutPageProps) =\u003e {\n  const user = await getUser();\n  if(!user) return {\n    error: true,\n    message: \"Please login\"\n  };\n  const products = [];\n  const line_items = [];\n\n  for (const product of cart) {\n    const pro = await prisma.product.findFirst({\n      where: {\n        id: product.productId\n      }\n    });\n    if(pro) {\n      console.log(pro);\n      products.push(pro);\n      line_items.push({\n        price_data: {\n          currency: \"USD\",\n          product_data: {\n            name: pro.name,\n            images: [pro.image],\n          },\n          unit_amount: Math.round(Number(product.price) * 100),\n        },\n        quantity: 1,\n      })\n    }\n  };\n\n  if(products.find((e) =\u003e e?.requiredSubscriptionId \u0026\u0026 e?.requiredSubscriptionId.length \u003c 1) \u0026\u0026 products.length \u003e 1) return {\n    error: true,\n    message: \"When purchasing a subscription please only have the subscription in your cart.     {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\nexport async function getMonthPrayerTimes({\n  month,\n  year,\n  masjidId\n}: {\n    month: number;\n    year: number;\n    masjidId: string;\n}) {\n  const prayerTimes = await prisma.prayerTime.findMany({\n    where: {\n      masjidId,\n      month: String(month),\n      year\n    },\n    orderBy: {\n      date: \"asc\"\n    }\n  });\n\n  return prayerTimes || [];\n}",

        "contents": "\n\nasync function getPrayerTimings(masjidId: string) {\n  const prayerTimings = await prisma.prayerTime.findMany({\n    where: {\n      masjidId,\n    },\n    orderBy: {\n      date: \"asc\"\n    }\n  })\n  return prayerTimings.length \u003c 2 ?     {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\nexport const getTotalDonationsThisMonth = async (masjidId?: string) =\u003e {\n  const now = new Date();\n  const start = startOfMonth(now);\n  const end = endOfMonth(now);\n  const where: any = {\n    createdAt: { gte: start, lte: end },\n    status: \"completed\",\n  };\n  if (masjidId) where.masjidId = masjidId;\n  const result = await prisma.donation.aggregate({\n    _sum: { amount: true },\n    where,\n  });\n  return result._sum.amount || 0;\n};",
        "detailedLines": [

          },

        "originalContents": "\n\nexport const getTotalDonationsThisMonth = async (masjidId?: string) =\u003e {\n  const now = new Date();\n  const start = startOfMonth(now);\n  const end = endOfMonth(now);\n  const where: any = {\n    createdAt: { gte: start, lte: end },\n    status: \"completed\",\n  };\n  if (masjidId) where.masjidId = masjidId;\n  const result = await prisma.donation.aggregate({\n    _sum: { amount: true },\n    where,\n  });\n  return result._sum.amount || 0;\n};",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n// Function to get related products (excluding the current product)\nexport async function getRelatedProducts(id: string, limit = 3): Promise\u003cProduct[]\u003e {\n  const products = await getProducts();\n  const product = products.find((e) =\u003e e.id === id);\n\n  if(!product) return [];\n\n  return products\n    .filter((p) =\u003e p.id !== id)\n    .filter((p) =\u003e p.category === product.category)\n    .slice(0, limit);\n}",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "// Function to get related products (excluding the current product)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "export async function getRelatedProducts(id: string, limit = 3): Promise\u003cProduct[]\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "  const products = await getProducts();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,

        "originalContents": "\n\n// Function to get related products (excluding the current product)\nexport async function getRelatedProducts(id: string, limit = 3): Promise\u003cProduct[]\u003e {\n  const products = await getProducts();\n  const product = products.find((e) =\u003e e.id === id);\n\n  if(!product) return [];\n\n  return products\n    .filter((p) =\u003e p.id !== id)\n    .filter((p) =\u003e p.category === product.category)\n    .slice(0, limit);\n}",

    const fetchOrder = async () =&gt; {
-     if(!id) return router.push("/dashboard/orders");
-     const orderData = await getOrderById(id as string);
-     console.log(orderData);
-     if(!orderData) return router.push("/dashboard/orders");
-     setOrder(orderData);
-     setCart(JSON.parse(orderData.cart));
-     setMasjid(orderData.masjid);
-     setStatus(orderData.status);
-     setTracking(orderData.trackingNumber || "");
-     if(orderData.stripeSessionId) {
-       if(orderData.stripeSessionId.includes("cs_")) {
-         const payment = await getSessionAndOrder(orderData.stripeSessionId);
-         setPaymentInfo(payment.stripeSession);
-       } else {

-         setPaymentInfo(payment.stripeSession);
-       }

+         console.log('No order ID provided');
+         return router.push("/dashboard/orders");
+       }
+       console.log('Fetching order with ID:', id);
+       const orderData = await getOrderById(id as string);
+       console.log('Order data received:', orderData);
+       
+       if(!orderData) {
+         console.log('No order data found');
+         return router.push("/dashboard/orders");
+       }
+       
+       setOrder(orderData);
+       setCart(JSON.parse(orderData.cart));
+       setMasjid(orderData.masjid);
+       setStatus(orderData.status);
+       setTracking(orderData.trackingNumber || "");
+       
+       if(orderData.stripeSessionId) {
+         try {
+           if(orderData.stripeSessionId.includes("cs_")) {
+             const payment = await getSessionAndOrder(orderData.stripeSessionId);
+             setPaymentInfo(payment.stripeSession);
+           } else {
+             const payment = await getPaymentAndOrder(orderData.stripeSessionId);
+             setPaymentInfo(payment.stripeSession);
+           }
+         } catch (error) {
+           console.error('Error fetching payment info:', error);
+         }
+       }
+     } catch (error) {
+       console.error('Error in fetchOrder:', error);
+       router.push("/dashboard/orders");
      }
    }

_**Agent**_

I've added comprehensive error handling and logging to both the server action and client component. This will help us identify where exactly the process is failing. Here's what I've done:

1. Added try-catch blocks with detailed error logging
2. Added console logs at key points to track the flow of data
3. Improved error handling for the payment info fetching
4. Added more descriptive error messages

To debug the issue:

1. Open your browser's developer console (F12 or right-click -> Inspect -> Console)
2. Try to load the order page again
3. Look for the console logs we've added, which will show:
   - When the fetch starts
   - The order ID being used
   - Whether the order was found
   - Any errors that occur during the process

The logs will help us identify if:
- The order ID is being passed correctly
- The database query is executing
- The order data is being returned
- Any errors are occurring during the process

Could you try loading the page again and let me know what logs you see in the console? This will help us pinpoint exactly where the process is getting stuck.

Also, I notice there's a linter error about the `admin` property not existing on the user type. This is a separate issue that we can address once we get the order loading working.

        "originalContents": "\n\n  return (\n    \u003cdiv className=\"max-w-2xl mx-auto pb-10\"\u003e\n      \u003ch2 className=\"text-3xl font-bold mb-6 text-[#550C18]\"\u003eCreate New Product\u003c/h2\u003e\n      \u003cform onSubmit={handleSubmit(onSubmit)} className=\"space-y-6\"\u003e\n        \u003cdiv className=\"space-y-2\"\u003e\n          \u003cLabel\u003eProduct Name\u003c/Label\u003e\n          \u003cInput {...register(\"name\", { required: \"Product name is required\" })} placeholder=\"Enter product name\" /\u003e\n          {errors.name \u0026\u0026 \u003cspan className=\"text-red-500 text-xs\"\u003e{errors.name.message as string}\u003c/span\u003e}\n        \u003c/div\u003e\n        \u003cdiv className=\"space-y-2\"\u003e\n          \u003cLabel\u003eDescription (Markdown Supported)\u003c/Label\u003e\n          \u003cdiv style={{ minHeight: 200 }}\u003e\n            {/* @ts-ignore */}\n            \u003cController\n              name=\"description\"\n              control={control}\n              render={({ field }) =\u003e \u003cMDEditor {...field} /\u003e}\n            /\u003e\n          \u003c/div\u003e\n        \u003c/div\u003e",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\nexport default function CreateProductPage() {\n  const router = useRouter()\n  const form = useForm({\n    defaultValues: {\n      name: \"\",\n      description: \"\",\n      features: [] as string[],\n      images: [] as ProductImagePreview[],\n      category: \"\",\n      type: \"kiosk\",\n      price: \"\",\n      discountType: \"\",\n      discountValue: \"\",\n      discountStart: \"\",\n      discountEnd: \"\",\n      url: \"\",\n      popular: false,\n      meta_data: {},\n    },\n  })\n  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false)\n  const { control, register, handleSubmit, setValue, watch, reset, formState: { errors } } = form\n  const [imagePreviews, setImagePreviews] = useState\u003cProductImagePreview[]\u003e([])\n  const [isPopular, setIsPopular] = useState(false)\n  const features = watch(\"features\")\n\n  const handleImageUpload = (e: React.ChangeEvent\u003cHTMLInputElement\u003e) =\u003e {\n    const files = Array.from(e.target.files || [])\n    if (files.length === 0) return\n    setImagePreviews((prev) =\u003e [\n      ...prev,\n      ...files.map((file, i) =\u003e ({\n        file,\n        url: URL.createObjectURL(file),\n        alt: \"\",\n        order: prev.length + i,\n      })),\n    ])\n  }",

        "originalContents": "\n\nexport default function CreateProductPage() {\n  const router = useRouter()\n  const form = useForm({\n    defaultValues: {\n      name: \"\",\n      description: \"\",\n      features: [] as string[],\n      images: [] as ProductImagePreview[],\n      category: \"\",\n      type: \"kiosk\",\n      price: \"\",\n      discountType: \"\",\n      discountValue: \"\",\n      discountStart: \"\",\n      discountEnd: \"\",\n      url: \"\",\n      popular: false,\n      meta_data: {},\n    },\n  })\n  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false)\n  const { control, register, handleSubmit, setValue, watch, reset, formState: { errors } } = form\n  const [imagePreviews, setImagePreviews] = useState\u003cProductImagePreview[]\u003e([])\n  const [isPopular, setIsPopular] = useState(false)\n  const features = watch(\"features\")\n\n  const handleImageUpload = (e: React.ChangeEvent\u003cHTMLInputElement\u003e) =\u003e {\n    const files = Array.from(e.target.files || [])\n    if (files.length === 0) return\n    setImagePreviews((prev) =\u003e [\n      ...prev,\n      ...files.map((file, i) =\u003e ({\n        file,\n        url: URL.createObjectURL(file),\n        alt: \"\",\n        order: prev.length + i,\n      })),\n    ])\n  }",

            "line": 63

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "export default function CreateProductPage() {\n\n\n  ...\n\n\n  const onSubmit = async (data: any) =\u003e {\n    setLoading(true)\n    try {\n      // Upload images if any\n      let uploadedImages: { url: string; alt: string; order: number }[] = []\n      if (imagePreviews.length \u003e 0) {\n        const formData = new FormData()\n        imagePreviews.forEach((img) =\u003e formData.append(\"files\", img.file))\n        const res = await fetch(\"/api/uploadthing\", {\n          method: \"POST\",\n          body: formData,\n        })\n        if (!res.ok) throw new Error(\"Failed to upload images\"), setLoading(false);\n        const uploaded = await res.json()\n        uploadedImages = uploaded.map((img: any, i: number) =\u003e ({\n          url: img.data.ufsUrl,\n          alt: imagePreviews[i]?.alt || \"\",\n          order: imagePreviews[i]?.order || i,\n        }));\n      }\n      // Prepare payload\n      const payload = {\n        ...data,\n        price: Number(data.price),\n        images: uploadedImages,\n        popular: isPopular,\n        discountType: data.discountType,\n        discountValue: Number(data.discountValue),\n        discountStart: data.discountStart,\n        discountEnd: data.discountEnd,\n        url: data.url,\n        features: data.features,\n        category: data.category,\n        type: data.type,\n        meta_data: data.meta_data,\n      }\n      const res = await fetch(\"/api/products\", {\n        method: \"POST\",\n        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n        body: JSON.stringify(payload),\n      })\n      if (!res.ok) {\n        const err = await res.json();\n        setLoading(false)\n        throw new Error(err.message || \"Failed to create product\")\n      }\n      toast({ title: \"Product Created!\", description: \"Product created successfully.\" })\n      reset()\n      setImagePreviews([])\n      router.push(\"/dashboard/products/manage\")\n    } catch (err: any) {\n      setLoading(false);\n      toast({ title: \"Error\", description: err.message || \"Failed to create product\", variant: \"destructive\" })\n    }\n  }",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\"use client\"\n\nimport { useState } from \"react\"\nimport { useForm, Controller } from \"react-hook-form\"\nimport { useRouter } from \"next/navigation\"\nimport { Input } from \"@/components/ui/input\"\nimport { Label } from \"@/components/ui/label\"\nimport { Button } from \"@/components/ui/button\"\nimport { Switch } from \"@/components/ui/switch\"\nimport { Select, SelectContent, SelectItem, SelectTrigger, SelectValue } from \"@/components/ui/select\"\nimport dynamic from \"next/dynamic\"\nimport { toast } from \"@/components/ui/use-toast\"\nimport { Loader2 } from \"lucide-react\"\n\n// @ts-ignore\nconst MDEditor = dynamic(() =\u003e import(\"@uiw/react-md-editor\"), { ssr: false })\n\ninterface ProductImagePreview {\n  file: File;\n  url: string;\n  alt: string;\n  order: number;\n}",
        "detailedLines": [

            "text": "\"use client\""

            "text": "import { Input } from \"@/components/ui/input\""
          },

            "lineNumber": 7,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "import { Button } from \"@/components/ui/button\""
          },

        "originalContents": "\"use client\"\n\nimport { useState } from \"react\"\nimport { useForm, Controller } from \"react-hook-form\"\nimport { useRouter } from \"next/navigation\"\nimport { Input } from \"@/components/ui/input\"\nimport { Label } from \"@/components/ui/label\"\nimport { Button } from \"@/components/ui/button\"\nimport { Switch } from \"@/components/ui/switch\"\nimport { Select, SelectContent, SelectItem, SelectTrigger, SelectValue } from \"@/components/ui/select\"\nimport dynamic from \"next/dynamic\"\nimport { toast } from \"@/components/ui/use-toast\"\nimport { Loader2 } from \"lucide-react\"\n\n// @ts-ignore\nconst MDEditor = dynamic(() =\u003e import(\"@uiw/react-md-editor\"), { ssr: false })\n\ninterface ProductImagePreview {\n  file: File;\n  url: string;\n  alt: string;\n  order: number;\n}",

        "contents": "export default function CreateProductPage() {\n\n        ...\n\n        \u003cdiv className=\"space-y-2\"\u003e\n          \u003cLabel\u003eProduct Type\u003c/Label\u003e\n          \u003cController\n            name=\"type\"\n            control={control}\n            render={({ field }) =\u003e (\n              \u003cSelect value={field.value} onValueChange={field.onChange}\u003e\n                \u003cSelectTrigger\u003e\n                  \u003cSelectValue placeholder=\"Select type\" /\u003e\n                \u003c/SelectTrigger\u003e\n                \u003cSelectContent\u003e\n                  \u003cSelectItem value=\"kiosk\"\u003eKiosk\u003c/SelectItem\u003e\n                  \u003cSelectItem value=\"service\"\u003eService\u003c/SelectItem\u003e\n                  \u003cSelectItem value=\"physical\"\u003ePhysical Product\u003c/SelectItem\u003e\n                \u003c/SelectContent\u003e\n              \u003c/Select\u003e\n            )}\n          /\u003e\n        \u003c/div\u003e\n        \u003cdiv className=\"space-y-2\"\u003e\n          \u003cLabel\u003ePrice\u003c/Label\u003e\n          \u003cInput type=\"number\" {...register(\"price\", { required: \"Price is required\" })} placeholder=\"0.00\" /\u003e\n          {errors.price \u0026\u0026 \u003cspan className=\"text-red-500 text-xs\"\u003e{errors.price.message as string}\u003c/span\u003e}\n        \u003c/div\u003e",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "export default function CreateProductPage() {\n\n        ...\n\n        \u003cdiv className=\"space-y-2\"\u003e\n          \u003cLabel\u003eFeatures (comma separated)\u003c/Label\u003e\n          \u003cController\n            name=\"features\"\n            control={control}\n            render={({ field }) =\u003e (\n              \u003cInput\n                placeholder=\"e.g. Fast, Secure, Easy to use\"\n                value={field.value?.join(\", \")}\n                onChange={e =\u003e field.onChange(e.target.value.split(\",\").map(f =\u003e f.trim()))}\n              /\u003e\n            )}\n          /\u003e\n        \u003c/div\u003e\n        \u003cButton className=\"w-full bg-[#550C18] hover:bg-[#78001A] text-white\" type=\"submit\" disabled={loading}\u003e\n          {loading \u0026\u0026 \u003cLoader2 className=\"w-4 h-4 mr-2 animate-spin\" /\u003e}\n          {loading ? \"Creating...\" : \"Create Product\"}\n        \u003c/Button\u003e\n      \u003c/form\u003e\n    \u003c/div\u003e\n  )\n}",

        "originalContents": "\n        \u003cdiv className=\"space-y-2\"\u003e\n          \u003cLabel\u003eFeatures (comma separated)\u003c/Label\u003e\n          \u003cController\n            name=\"features\"\n            control={control}\n            render={({ field }) =\u003e (\n              \u003cInput\n                placeholder=\"e.g. Fast, Secure, Easy to use\"\n                value={field.value?.join(\", \")}\n                onChange={e =\u003e field.onChange(e.target.value.split(\",\").map(f =\u003e f.trim()))}\n              /\u003e\n            )}\n          /\u003e\n        \u003c/div\u003e\n        \u003cButton className=\"w-full bg-[#550C18] hover:bg-[#78001A] text-white\" type=\"submit\" disabled={loading}\u003e\n          {loading \u0026\u0026 \u003cLoader2 className=\"w-4 h-4 mr-2 animate-spin\" /\u003e}\n          {loading ? \"Creating...\" : \"Create Product\"}\n        \u003c/Button\u003e\n      \u003c/form\u003e\n    \u003c/div\u003e\n  )\n}",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "export default function ManageProductsPage() {\n\n\n  ...\n\n\n  const onSubmit = async (data: any) =\u003e {\n    try {\n      // Upload images if any\n      let uploadedImages: { url: string; alt: string; order: number }[] = []\n      if (imagePreviews.length \u003e 0) {\n        const formData = new FormData()\n        imagePreviews.forEach((img) =\u003e formData.append(\"files\", img.file))\n        const res = await fetch(\"/api/uploadthing\", {\n          method: \"POST\",\n          body: formData,\n        })\n        if (!res.ok) throw new Error(\"Failed to upload images\")\n        const uploaded = await res.json()\n        uploadedImages = uploaded.map((img: any, i: number) =\u003e ({\n          url: img.url,\n          alt: imagePreviews[i]?.alt || \"\",\n          order: imagePreviews[i]?.order || i,\n        }))\n      }\n      // Prepare payload\n      const payload = {\n        ...data,\n        price: Number(data.price),\n        images: uploadedImages,\n      }\n      const res = await fetch(\"/api/products\", {\n        method: \"POST\",\n        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n        body: JSON.stringify(payload),\n      })\n      if (!res.ok) {\n        const err = await res.json()\n        throw new Error(err.message || \"Failed to create product\")\n      }\n      toast({ title: \"Product Created!\", description: \"Product created successfully.\" })\n      setIsCreateDialogOpen(false)\n      reset()\n      setImagePreviews([])\n      fetchProducts()\n    } catch (err: any) {\n      toast({ title: \"Error\", description: err.message || \"Failed to create product\", variant: \"destructive\" })\n    }\n  }",

        "originalContents": "\n\n  const onSubmit = async (data: any) =\u003e {\n    try {\n      // Upload images if any\n      let uploadedImages: { url: string; alt: string; order: number }[] = []\n      if (imagePreviews.length \u003e 0) {\n        const formData = new FormData()\n        imagePreviews.forEach((img) =\u003e formData.append(\"files\", img.file))\n        const res = await fetch(\"/api/uploadthing\", {\n          method: \"POST\",\n          body: formData,\n        })\n        if (!res.ok) throw new Error(\"Failed to upload images\")\n        const uploaded = await res.json()\n        uploadedImages = uploaded.map((img: any, i: number) =\u003e ({\n          url: img.url,\n          alt: imagePreviews[i]?.alt || \"\",\n          order: imagePreviews[i]?.order || i,\n        }))\n      }\n      // Prepare payload\n      const payload = {\n        ...data,\n        price: Number(data.price),\n        images: uploadedImages,\n      }\n      const res = await fetch(\"/api/products\", {\n        method: \"POST\",\n        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n        body: JSON.stringify(payload),\n      })\n      if (!res.ok) {\n        const err = await res.json()\n        throw new Error(err.message || \"Failed to create product\")\n      }\n      toast({ title: \"Product Created!\", description: \"Product created successfully.\" })\n      setIsCreateDialogOpen(false)\n      reset()\n      setImagePreviews([])\n      fetchProducts()\n    } catch (err: any) {\n      toast({ title: \"Error\", description: err.message || \"Failed to create product\", variant: \"destructive\" })\n    }\n  }",
